full_text:
Tumor tissue was cut and dissociated using Dispase. For 20 mL of AdF+++ medium, I used 400 uL Dispase, and 200 uL DNAse. Incubated in the oven for 45 min; RBC lysis buffer 2 mL + 10 mL of AdF+++ medium. After centrifuge, I resuspended cells in 1 mL of AdF+++, diluted 1:10, and counted cells.

Dispase dissociated cells:

Cell count/ml 1):

total: 2.44 E5

live: 1,62 E5

viability: 66.4 %

size: 8.0 um

Cell count /ml 2):

total: 1.77 E5

live: 1.29 E5

viability: 73.3 %

size: 8.3 um

Total cell count: 2.1 E6

Total volume: 1 mL

Overall # of cells in 1 mL was 2.1 E6.

I took 0.5 E6 cells for flow cytometry staining, and the rest was seeded.

A). Drug treatment for flow cytometry (48 well plate)

8 conditions, 6 replicates/condition = 48 wells

48 wells * 40 uL = 1920 uL -> 2001

Since the omentum gel starts polymerizing as soon as the aprotinin is added, it is better to make three separate tubes each one of 667 uL (approx. 2001 uL/3) and not everything at once in a single tube.

Since there’s not enough cells to seed at a 1000 cells/uL density, I will keep it at 800 cells/uL, so the total number of cells per tube that will be

needed is: 800 cells/uL * 667 uL = 0.5336 E6 cells/tube. Therefore, 250 uL from the original cell suspension are needed in each of the three tubes.

ORG full medium → 302.28

Cells → 250 uL

ome-gel → 97.38 µl

aprotinin → 2.001 µl

thrombin → 2.001 µl

fibrinogen → 13.34 µl

Plate 40 uL per well in 48 well plate (see the map below)→ Let it solidify for 60 min - add 350 uL medium + 50 ul of each of the diluted drugs (anti-PD1, anti-TIGIT) per well next day

After one day, the drugs were added accordingly to the map.

9X stocks were prepared in AdF+++ medium supplemented with cytokines. Below are the calculations to explain the preparation of these stocks.

Reagent | Stock concentration | Working concentration | Ratio
Omentum gel | 17.1 mg/mL | 2.5 mg/mL | 0.146
Aprotinin | 10 mg/mL | 33.3 ug/mL | 0.003
Thrombin | 100 U | 0.3 U | 0.003
Fibrinogen | 25 ug/mL | 0.5 ug/mL | 0.02
